candidates.direction_name: Trim PTX Microkernel Barriers On The Restored 128x128 Anchor
ranking_notes: This is the strongest next move that is both directly evidenced by the last failed occupancy probes and still compatible with the restored anchor.
It is preferred over another broad occupancy edit because the recent evidence says synchronization became the binding constraint as soon as occupancy improved.
candidates.hypothesis: The loop is back on the exact PTX anchor, and the last two occupancy probes already answered the register-only question: both the PTX and non-PTX 128x128 launch-bounds variants cut registers to about 168 and raised active warps to roughly 24.7%, but both still regressed because synchronization cost spiked into the ~11% band. The accepted anchor keeps the hot-band kernel at 22.016 KB shared memory per block, 200 registers per thread, 16.63% active warps, 7.23% long-scoreboard stall, and 5.49% barrier stall. The next coherent move is therefore to keep the restored 128x128 PTX surface intact and surgically trim the wait/sync cadence or export handoff inside the PTX microkernel, without reintroducing the larger x32 shared-memory footprint.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Barrier cadence and PTX export/control handoff inside the single-K 128x128 PTX hot-band microkernel, now that recovery is complete and the failed residency probes isolated synchronization as the limiting tax on higher occupancy.
candidates.risk: High. This is microkernel-level control-flow surgery on the best-known branch, so correctness and codegen stability are both fragile.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the edit inflates shared memory above the restored 22.016 KB footprint, or if barrier stall does not improve materially while runtime stays flat or regresses.

candidates.direction_name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit
ranking_notes: Keep one bounded same-surface exploit family alive because the current best run still has low active-warps and meaningful long-scoreboard slack.
It ranks below dir_01 because the recent occupancy probes provide stronger new evidence for synchronization-focused surgery than for a generic PTX cleanup.
candidates.hypothesis: Even without another occupancy push, the restored PTX anchor still leaves obvious local waste on the table: the hot-band kernel dominates device time at 32.684512 ms with 200 registers per thread, only 16.63% active warps, 48.41% tensor-pipe activity, and 7.23% long-scoreboard stall. A bounded exploit pass inside the accepted PTX one-K control and export path remains coherent as a smaller-scope fallback to barrier surgery. The goal is not to replay the already-rejected handoff variants, but to trim residual consume-order and export-control friction while preserving the current geometry and grouped traversal.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Residual control-path overhead and live-range pressure inside the accepted PTX one-K 128x128 hot-band branch.
candidates.risk: Moderate. This stays on the winning family and should be more bounded than the barrier-surgery branch, but PTX control-path edits are still correctness-sensitive.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the edit collapses into a replay of a previously measured PTX handoff variant or if it fails to move either long-scoreboard stall or end-to-end runtime.

candidates.direction_name: Transplant The Half-Panel Register Budget Into The Correct 256x128 Pivot
ranking_notes: Keep this family alive because it still offers the highest theoretical ceiling in the repo.
It ranks below both PTX-local follow-ups because the implementation surface is broader and the correctness history is materially worse.
candidates.hypothesis: The 256x128 family still represents the largest visible occupancy ceiling in repo history, because its broken half-panel branch showed that this shape can reach roughly low-90s registers per thread and low-30% active warps when the staging path is aggressive enough. The reason it stays third is that the correctness-safe 256x128 pivot path is still much broader and riskier than the PTX-local follow-ups. It remains worth carrying in the queue because it is the only family with a credible path to break the long-running 16.6% active-warps plateau decisively.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register footprint and synchronization strategy on the 256x128 hot-band path, plus correctness-sensitive writer ownership.
candidates.risk: High. This is still the broadest and most correctness-sensitive active family in the repo.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately on correctness failure or on another 28-30 ms class regression without a decisive occupancy breakthrough.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
